main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Inversion-based Inference Perturbation (IIP) framework","Unconditional DDIM inversion to obtain semantic latent codes","Perturbation of inference procedure with optimized random prompt embeddings"],"problems":["Image-level memorization detection in text-to-image diffusion models without access to original prompts or metadata","Detecting whether a specific image (not prompt) has been memorized by a diffusion model, including partial or whole memorization","Robust detection of memorized images even under data augmentation attacks"],"datasets":["Curated datasets simulating realistic memorization scenarios (details not specified in the provided text)","Various datasets across three memorization settings: memorized vs non-memorized training images, memorized training vs non-training images, generated memorized vs non-memorized images"],"metrics":["Similarity of original images after inference perturbation","Magnitude of text-conditional noise prediction (TCNP/MTCNP) after perturbation","State-of-the-art performance (likely measured by accuracy, AUROC, or similar, though not explicitly stated)"],"results":[{"metric":"State-of-the-art performance","value":"IIP achieves state-of-the-art results in detecting memorized images across various datasets and settings, including robustness to data augmentation attacks (exact quantitative values not provided in the text)"}],"novelty_claims":["First to formalize and address image-level memorization detection for text-to-image diffusion models without requiring original prompts or metadata.","Proposes the IIP framework leveraging unconditional DDIM inversion and inference perturbation with random prompts for effective detection.","Uncovers two new characteristics of memorized images under inference perturbation: lower similarity and higher TCNP magnitude.","Demonstrates strong robustness of the method against data augmentation attacks."]}

methods: Inversion-based Inference Perturbation (IIP) framework
Unconditional DDIM inversion to obtain semantic latent codes
Perturbation of inference procedure with optimized random prompt embeddings
problems: Image-level memorization detection in text-to-image diffusion models without access to original prompts or metadata
Detecting whether a specific image (not prompt) has been memorized by a diffusion model, including partial or whole memorization
Robust detection of memorized images even under data augmentation attacks
datasets: Curated datasets simulating realistic memorization scenarios (details not specified in the provided text)
Various datasets across three memorization settings: memorized vs non-memorized training images, memorized training vs non-training images, generated memorized vs non-memorized images
metrics: Similarity of original images after inference perturbation
Magnitude of text-conditional noise prediction (TCNP/MTCNP) after perturbation
State-of-the-art performance (likely measured by accuracy, AUROC, or similar, though not explicitly stated)

results.value: IIP achieves state-of-the-art results in detecting memorized images across various datasets and settings, including robustness to data augmentation attacks (exact quantitative values not provided in the text)

novelty_claims: First to formalize and address image-level memorization detection for text-to-image diffusion models without requiring original prompts or metadata.
Proposes the IIP framework leveraging unconditional DDIM inversion and inference perturbation with random prompts for effective detection.
Uncovers two new characteristics of memorized images under inference perturbation: lower similarity and higher TCNP magnitude.
Demonstrates strong robustness of the method against data augmentation attacks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Analysis of cross-attention behavior in text-to-image diffusion models to understand memorization","A detection approach for memorization based on quantification of cross-attention behavior (attention score analysis)","An inference-time mitigation method that reduces memorization by adjusting attention dispersion","A training-time mitigation method that reduces memorization by adjusting attention dispersion"],"problems":["Memorization of training images and prompts in text-to-image diffusion models","Potential copyright infringement and privacy risks due to memorization in diffusion models","Lack of understanding of the mechanisms (specifically cross-attention) underlying memorization in diffusion models"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Provides a novel perspective on memorization in diffusion models by analyzing the role of cross-attention mechanisms","Empirically reveals that memorization is associated with cross-attention focusing disproportionately on specific token embeddings","Introduces a simple and efficient detection method for memorization based on attention score analysis, requiring no repeated or additional inference operations","Proposes both inference-time and training-time mitigation strategies that reduce memorization by adjusting attention dispersion, without compromising image quality or generation speed"]}

methods: Analysis of cross-attention behavior in text-to-image diffusion models to understand memorization
A detection approach for memorization based on quantification of cross-attention behavior (attention score analysis)
An inference-time mitigation method that reduces memorization by adjusting attention dispersion
A training-time mitigation method that reduces memorization by adjusting attention dispersion
problems: Memorization of training images and prompts in text-to-image diffusion models
Potential copyright infringement and privacy risks due to memorization in diffusion models
Lack of understanding of the mechanisms (specifically cross-attention) underlying memorization in diffusion models
novelty_claims: Provides a novel perspective on memorization in diffusion models by analyzing the role of cross-attention mechanisms
Empirically reveals that memorization is associated with cross-attention focusing disproportionately on specific token embeddings
Introduces a simple and efficient detection method for memorization based on attention score analysis, requiring no repeated or additional inference operations
Proposes both inference-time and training-time mitigation strategies that reduce memorization by adjusting attention dispersion, without compromising image quality or generation speed

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Anti-Memorization Guidance (AMG) framework","Desspecification guidance (G_spe)","Caption deduplication guidance (G_dup)","Dissimilarity guidance (G_sim)","Automatic detection system for potential memorization during inference"],"problems":["Memorization and regurgitation of training data by pretrained diffusion models during inference","Litigation and copyright risks due to memorization in generative models","Lack of effective methods to eliminate memorization without degrading image quality or text alignment","Absence of automated detection and targeted mitigation of memorization cases"],"datasets":["CIFAR-10","LAION"],"metrics":["FID (Fréchet Inception Distance)","CLIP score"],"results":[{"metric":"Memorization instances","value":"All instances eradicated with AMG"},{"metric":"Image quality (FID)","value":"No or marginal impact compared to baseline"},{"metric":"Text-alignment (CLIP score)","value":"No or marginal impact compared to baseline"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of the Anti-Memorization Guidance (AMG) framework with three targeted guidance strategies addressing the main causes of memorization in diffusion models.","First approach to fully eradicate all instances of memorization in pretrained diffusion models with no or marginal impact on image quality and text alignment.","AMG features an innovative automatic detection system for potential memorization during inference, enabling selective and minimal intervention.","First to identify and address memorization in class-conditional diffusion model generations, in addition to unconditional and text-conditional settings."]}

methods: Anti-Memorization Guidance (AMG) framework
Desspecification guidance (G_spe)
Caption deduplication guidance (G_dup)
Dissimilarity guidance (G_sim)
Automatic detection system for potential memorization during inference
problems: Memorization and regurgitation of training data by pretrained diffusion models during inference
Litigation and copyright risks due to memorization in generative models
Lack of effective methods to eliminate memorization without degrading image quality or text alignment
Absence of automated detection and targeted mitigation of memorization cases

results.value: All instances eradicated with AMG
No or marginal impact compared to baseline
No or marginal impact compared to baseline

novelty_claims: Introduction of the Anti-Memorization Guidance (AMG) framework with three targeted guidance strategies addressing the main causes of memorization in diffusion models.
First approach to fully eradicate all instances of memorization in pretrained diffusion models with no or marginal impact on image quality and text alignment.
AMG features an innovative automatic detection system for potential memorization during inference, enabling selective and minimal intervention.
First to identify and address memorization in class-conditional diffusion model generations, in addition to unconditional and text-conditional settings.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["EDICT (Exact Diffusion Inversion via Coupled Transformations)","DDIM inversion (Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models inversion, as baseline)"],"problems":["Inversion in denoising diffusion models (finding the initial noise vector that produces a given input image)","Instability and inaccuracy of DDIM inversion for real images, leading to poor image reconstruction and loss of content","Enabling robust and high-fidelity real image editing using diffusion models"],"datasets":["MS-COCO","ImageNet 2012 validation set"],"metrics":["Mean Square Error (MSE) of reconstruction"],"results":[{"metric":"Mean Square Error (MSE) improvement (COCO dataset)","value":"EDICT improves MSE of reconstruction by a factor of two over DDIM"}],"novelty_claims":["EDICT enables mathematically exact inversion of real and model-generated images in diffusion models.","EDICT uses two coupled noise vectors inspired by affine coupling layers to achieve exact inversion, unlike previous methods relying on local linearization.","EDICT does not require model training, finetuning, prompt tuning, or extra data, and can be combined with any pretrained diffusion model.","EDICT significantly outperforms DDIM inversion in reconstructing real images, especially on complex datasets like MS-COCO."]}

methods: EDICT (Exact Diffusion Inversion via Coupled Transformations)
DDIM inversion (Denoising Diffusion Implicit Models inversion, as baseline)
problems: Inversion in denoising diffusion models (finding the initial noise vector that produces a given input image)
Instability and inaccuracy of DDIM inversion for real images, leading to poor image reconstruction and loss of content
Enabling robust and high-fidelity real image editing using diffusion models

results.value: EDICT improves MSE of reconstruction by a factor of two over DDIM

novelty_claims: EDICT enables mathematically exact inversion of real and model-generated images in diffusion models.
EDICT uses two coupled noise vectors inspired by affine coupling layers to achieve exact inversion, unlike previous methods relying on local linearization.
EDICT does not require model training, finetuning, prompt tuning, or extra data, and can be combined with any pretrained diffusion model.
EDICT significantly outperforms DDIM inversion in reconstructing real images, especially on complex datasets like MS-COCO.

results.metric: Reconstruction accuracy (foreground object from background crop, test image)
results.value: Low (only general category inferred, not specific object)

results.metric: Effect of training epochs on déjà vu memorization
results.value: Memorization increases with more training epochs

results.metric: Effect of dataset size on déjà vu memorization
results.value: Memorization persists even with large datasets (up to half of ImageNet)

novelty_claims: First systematic study of unintended memorization (déjà vu memorization) in SSL models
Introduction of a novel data reconstruction pipeline for non-generative vision models
Demonstration that déjà vu memorization is not detected by standard representation quality metrics
Proposal of new metrics to quantify memorization in SSL models
Identification of privacy risks unique to SSL due to memorization of object-background associations

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["VL-Déjà-Vu: a new method for measuring memorization in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by comparing the fraction of ground-truth objects in an image that can be predicted from its text description and retrieved images","Individual sample-level test for detecting memorization in specific image-text pairs","Aggregate population-level test for measuring memorization across a dataset","Mitigation via text randomization/masking during training"],"problems":["Measuring memorization in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) beyond spurious correlations","Distinguishing true memorization from spurious correlations in VLMs trained on image-caption pairs","Evaluating the extent to which VLMs retain information about individual objects in training images","Mitigating memorization in VLMs to reduce information leakage"],"datasets":["Shutterstock (subsets, e.g., 40M images for training, 20M public set for evaluation)","Filtered LAION"],"metrics":["Fraction of ground-truth objects in the original image that co-occur in the retrieved image (object overlap metric)","Comparison of memorization between target VLM (trained on the image) and reference VLM (not trained on the image)","Impact on downstream task performance (utility)"],"results":[{"metric":"Object overlap (memorization) for target VLM vs reference VLM","value":"Target VLM recovers significantly more memorized objects than reference VLM (qualitative and quantitative results, e.g., Figure 1, Figure 11)"},{"metric":"Effect of text randomization on memorization","value":"Text randomization considerably mitigates memorization while only moderately impacting downstream task performance"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes VL-Déjà-Vu, a new method for measuring memorization in VLMs that distinguishes memorization from spurious correlation by comparing object overlap in retrieved images between models trained with and without a given image-text pair","Introduces both sample-level and population-level memorization tests for VLMs","Demonstrates that memorization occurs in VLMs even when they generalize well, across different training set sizes and regularization settings","Shows that text randomization/masking during training can significantly reduce memorization in VLMs with only marginal impact on utility"]}

methods: VL-Déjà-Vu: a new method for measuring memorization in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by comparing the fraction of ground-truth objects in an image that can be predicted from its text description and retrieved images
Individual sample-level test for detecting memorization in specific image-text pairs
Aggregate population-level test for measuring memorization across a dataset
Mitigation via text randomization/masking during training
problems: Measuring memorization in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) beyond spurious correlations
Distinguishing true memorization from spurious correlations in VLMs trained on image-caption pairs
Evaluating the extent to which VLMs retain information about individual objects in training images
Mitigating memorization in VLMs to reduce information leakage
datasets: Shutterstock (subsets, e.g., 40M images for training, 20M public set for evaluation)
metrics: Fraction of ground-truth objects in the original image that co-occur in the retrieved image (object overlap metric)
Comparison of memorization between target VLM (trained on the image) and reference VLM (not trained on the image)

results.metric: Object overlap (memorization) for target VLM vs reference VLM
results.value: Target VLM recovers significantly more memorized objects than reference VLM (qualitative and quantitative results, e.g., Figure 1, Figure 11)

results.metric: Effect of text randomization on memorization
results.value: Text randomization considerably mitigates memorization while only moderately impacting downstream task performance

novelty_claims: Proposes VL-Déjà-Vu, a new method for measuring memorization in VLMs that distinguishes memorization from spurious correlation by comparing object overlap in retrieved images between models trained with and without a given image-text pair
Introduces both sample-level and population-level memorization tests for VLMs
Demonstrates that memorization occurs in VLMs even when they generalize well, across different training set sizes and regularization settings
Shows that text randomization/masking during training can significantly reduce memorization in VLMs with only marginal impact on utility

methods: Iterative Inversion (IterInv) technique for pixel-level T2I models

Combining IterInv with DiffEdit for text-guided image editing
problems: Loss of image details in latent-space T2I models due to autoencoder compression
